one_time_description: Flood claim litigation settlement received from the Province of Ontario in July 2022 for $39,397,244; trust fund revenue of $11,597,336 also significantly above normal
economic_notes: First Nation receives OFNLP distributions (Casino Rama profits) restricted to community development, health, education, economic development and cultural development. Portfolio investments include Boundary Waters Anishinaabeg Development LP, Rainy Lake Tribal Resource Management, Nanicost Ltd., and Agency One LP. Flood claim settlement of $39.4M received from Province of Ontario in July 2022.
notes: Qualified audit opinion: (1) Agency One Trust not proportionately consolidated as required by PSAS — financial impact undetermined; (2) trust income distributions recorded on cash basis instead of accrual — revenue understated by $107,094 and receivables/accumulated surplus understated by $464,425. Major one-time item: flood claim litigation settlement of $39,397,244 received from Province of Ontario July 2022 — this drove the large trust fund revenue line of $11,597,336 and dramatic increase in accumulated surplus from $15.2M to $28.9M. 1468069 Ontario Limited o/a Gobe Corporation fully consolidated as subsidiary. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements provided. fiscal_year corrected to 2023 (year ended March 31, 2023). total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $6,370,752 + Ontario ministries ($113,478+$160,305+$27,935+$212,178+$100,000+$50,934+$296,457) + CMHC $150,000 + OFNLP $439,569 + Health Canada $0 = $7,921,608 plus Grand Council Treaty #3 $1,083,887 + Fort Frances Tribal Area Health Services $211,329 + Weechi-It-Te-Win $732,694 = $9,949,518; osr = total revenue minus government transfers.
